items.summary: Donald Trump arrives at next week’s NATO summit in Ankara holding unusual leverage, because the US decides which allies get access to the world’s most advanced AI, Politico reports. items.summary: Citigroup chief executive Jane Fraser says the financial sector is running two AI races at once, she told the South China Morning Post. items.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
items.summary: "Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. items.title: ByteDance and Alibaba kill custom AI companions as China’s new rules bite
items.summary: ByteDance’s Doubao and Alibaba’s Qwen, two of China’s biggest consumer AI apps, are disabling their customised agent features, the South China Morning Post reports. items.title: India orders Meta to remove Instagram ads promoting child abuse material
items.summary: India’s IT ministry has ordered Meta to immediately disable all Instagram advertisements and content promoting child sexual exploitation and abuse material, according to The Week. items.summary: South Korea will seek to create a “future response fund” financed by the tax windfall from its semiconductor boom, Yonhap reported.
